definition: CLO overcollateralization test mechanics create non-linear stress amplification at the contractual 7.5% CCC-bucket threshold, triggering mandatory cash diversion and step-function market disruption. A discrete, deterministic mechanism invisible to macro-only frameworks, partially validated by the 2020 COVID episode.
evidence: 7.5% CCC-bucket threshold triggers mandatory cash diversion as contractual fact
CLO reinvestment period expirations of $250-350B over 18 months as regime-dependent refinancing cliff
Structured credit ($2.7T in CLOs + private credit) implicitly short sustained inflation move
open_questions: Can enough simultaneous downgrades accumulate across the CLO market to trigger systemic effects?
How does Japanese CLO AAA demand create cross-border feedback?
